title: Method for a new risk assessment of urban inundation: G-DEMATEL‚ÄìAHP
author: Zheng

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Step 1: According to the data from the expert questionnaire survey and Eq.¬Ý(1)‚Äì(2), the direct influential relation matrix Ck‚Äô from the kth expert can be expressed using the following equation:(3)C‚Ä≤k=[0x12,k‚Ä¶x1n,kx21,k‚ã±‚Ä¶x2n,k‚ãÆ‚Ä¶0‚ãÆxn1,kxn2,k‚Ä¶0]n√ón

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Step 3: Fuzzification using Grey theory. The value of cij,k can be fuzzified into interval grey numbers according to Grey theory [3]. The corresponding relationship between the interval grey number and its operating algorithm are presented in Table 4 and Eq.¬Ý(3)‚Äì(5) in a companion study [1]. Therefore, the elements of matrix Ck can be transformed into the grey interval number as follows [4]:Ck‚áí[Ckdown,Ckup](5)[Ckdown,Ckup]=[0c12,kdown‚ãØc1n,kdownc21,kdown‚ã±‚ãØc2n,kdown‚ãÆ‚ãØ0‚ãÆcn1,kdowncn2,kdown‚ãØ0,0c12,kup‚ãØc1n,kupc21,kup‚ã±‚ãØc2n,kup‚ãÆ‚ãØ0‚ãÆcn1,kupcn2,kup‚ãØ0]n√ón

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Step 4: Averaging the comprehensive influential relation matrix (N), which is composed of the upper limit matrix (Nup) and lower limit matrix (Ndown), consisting of the average value cij of the upper and lower bounds from all experts‚Äô influence scores (cij,k), respectively. Eq.¬Ý(6) shows the comprehensive influential relation matrix using grey theory.(6)N=[Ndown,Nup]=[0c¬Ø12down‚ãØc¬Ø1ndownc¬Ø21down‚ã±‚ãØc¬Ø2ndown‚ãÆ‚ãØ0‚ãÆc¬Øn1downc¬Øn2down‚ãØ0,0c¬Ø12up‚ãØc¬Ø1nupc¬Ø21up‚ã±‚ãØc¬Ø2nup‚ãÆ‚ãØ0‚ãÆc¬Øn1upc¬Øn2up‚ãØ0]n√ón=([0,0][c¬Ø12down,c¬Ø12up]‚ãØ[c¬Ø1ndown,c¬Ø1nup][c¬Ø21down,c¬Ø21up]‚ã±‚ãØ[c¬Ø2ndown,c¬Ø2nup]‚ãÆ‚ãØ[0,0]‚ãÆ[c¬Øn1down,c¬Øn1up][c¬Øn2down,c¬Øn2up]‚Ä¶[0,0])n√ónwhere c¬Øijdown=‚àëk=1Kcij,kdownK, c¬Øijup=‚àëk=1Kcij,kupK, (7) where K is the total number of experts.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction:

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Step 6: Degree of influence of DEMATEL. Based on the results obtained using Eq.¬Ý(15), sij can compose a new crisp influential matrix (S), which can be expressed as Eq.¬Ý(16). Then, the total relation matrix (T) can be calculated using Eq.¬Ý(17). The influential degree (Di) and influenced degree (Ri) of the ith factor can be obtained from matrix T, as expressed in Eq.¬Ý(19).(16)S=[0s12‚Ä¶s1ns21‚ã±‚Ä¶s2n‚ãÆ‚Ä¶0‚ãÆsn1sn2‚Ä¶0]n√ón(17)T=Snor(E‚àíSnor)‚àí1

steps.instruction: Where(18)Snor=1max1‚â§i‚â§n‚àëjsij√óS,i,j=1,‚Ä¶,n.(19)Di=‚àëj=1nTij,Ri=‚àëi=1nTijStep 7: Weights using AHP. The comparison matrix (A) of the AHP was established by pairwise comparison of Di. Detailed information on the AHP calculation procedure can be found in a companion article [1]. The final weight (—°i) for each factor was obtained from this step.

steps.title: Application of the method
steps.instruction: The traditional AHP approach has the following limitations: (i) the judgements from experts have uncertainty [6], and (ii) choosing suitable scores from experts‚Äô scattered judgements is difficult [7]. Additionally, both AHP and DEMATEL require a questionnaire survey from experts. However, the typical pairwise comparison questionnaires of these two methods are different [8], [9], [10], [11], which makes the procedure of questionnaire evaluation repetitive and verbose [12], [13], [14], [15], [16], [17]. Therefore, the proposed method first simplified the expert questionnaire steps of DEMATEL and AHP through a nine-score questionnaire, and then combined these two MCDM methods with Grey theory to consider all experts‚Äô judgements and calculate the weights. Eq.¬Ý(20) lists the index layer Ni comparison matrix (ANi) for risk assessment of flooding disasters. The details of the risk assessment frame are indicated in a companion article [1].(20)ANi=(aij)n√ón=[1.001.051.130.951.001.070.890.931.001.290.740.781.230.700.741.150.660.690.770.810.871.351.421.521.291.351.451.000.570.601.751.001.051.660.951.00]6√ó6

steps.title: Application of the method
steps.instruction: Based on this method, the consistency ratio (CR) of the Ni comparison matrix can be checked, which should be less than 0.1. Meanwhile, the weight (wi) of each factor belonging to the index layer Ni can be computed using MATLAB software based on Eq. (7)‚Äì(9) in a companion article [1]. Therefore, the weights of the factors in the Ni layer were calculated as wi¬Ý=¬Ý(0.1600, 0.1523, 0.1420, 0.1236, 0.2163, and 0.2058).

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: To test the advantages of the proposed G-DEMTAL-AHP method, the weights of the factors with the corresponding actual data from Zhengzhou were mapped using GIS software. The distribution of risk levels could be obtained from this step, which was compared with the waterlogging sites that occurred during the Zhengzhou flooding disaster on 20 July 2021. Detailed information on dividing risk levels and relative analyses can be found in Section 4.2. of a companion article [1]. Moreover, the existing TFN-AHP method was used to evaluate the flooding risk level in the Zhengzhou case. The results of the weights and risk distribution of the two methods are presented in details in the companion article [1].
